d7b_reasoning: The strategy presents a coherent mean-reversion-to-momentum transition logic: entering on RSI crossing above oversold (30) when MACD histogram is positive, price exceeds EMA-12, and positive 1-hour return confirms immediate momentum. Exit logic splits between profit-taking (RSI>65 with 3% 24h gain) and momentum exhaustion (MACD histogram crossing negative while price drops below EMA-12). This dual-condition framework is economically defensible. Theme alignment is strong—all five factors (rsi_14, macd_hist, return_1h, return_24h, ema_12/close relationship) directly address momentum detection through oscillators, histogram signals, and short-term returns. However, structural variant risk is high: the factor set {close, ema_12, macd_hist, return_1h, return_24h, rsi_14} appears functionally identical to prior #52 {close, macd_hist, return_1h, return_24h, rsi_14, sma_50} and closely resembles #76 {close, ema_12, macd_hist, return_1h, return_24h, rsi_14, sma_20}, differing only in moving-average window choice. The core RSI-MACD-return combination with price-to-MA filtering has been extensively explored in the batch.
